full_prompt: Design a single-piece foam-core bolt-on Impact Attenuator for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 car. SUPRA 2026 Rulebook v1.1 (derived from collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark) requires the IA to have minimum dimensions 200 mm (length) x 100 mm (height) x 200 mm (width), and when mounted in front of the Anti-Intrusion Plate (AIP) on a 300 kg test mass impacted at 7.0 m/s, it must decelerate the mass with an average of no more than 20 g and a peak of no more than 40 g over any 3 ms window; energy absorbed must therefore exceed 7350 J. SUPRA 2026 in India uses a sled-test setup calibrated on a 1018 steel surface with coefficient of friction 0.12 (pre-measured) at ambient 28 C. This spec targets the foam-filled option: the attenuator is 200 mm (L) x 100 mm (H) x 200 mm (W) rectangular block of IMPAXX 700 closed-cell extruded polystyrene (or equivalent), density 29 kg/m^3, compressive plateau stress 0.31 MPa at 10 to 70 % strain, ultimate crush 70 % strain. The foam is wrapped in a 1.2 mm 6061-T6 aluminium skin that folds on two opposing faces; the skin provides bond interface to the AIP via a ring of 3M DP420 adhesive of 15 mm width around the perimeter. AIP is 1.5 mm 6061-T6 aluminium, 200 mm x 350 mm, bolted to the front bulkhead with 4 x M8 bolts on a 180 mm x 150 mm pattern. Load cases: LC1 (Regulatory frontal impact) — 300 kg mass at 7 m/s against rigid barrier; LC2 (Oblique 10 deg) — 300 kg at 7 m/s 10 deg off-axis; LC3 (Sled test correlation) — 300 kg at 6.5 m/s against a sled surface with 0.12 friction (India test rig geometry); LC4 (Bolt pull) — 4 kN steady tension per AIP bolt. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, average deceleration <= 20 g; (R2) under LC1, peak 3 ms deceleration <= 40 g; (R3) under LC1, energy absorbed >= 7350 J; (R4) under LC1, crush stroke <= 170 mm (keep 30 mm margin vs. full 200 mm); (R5) under LC2, peak deceleration <= 50 g, energy absorbed >= 6000 J; (R6) under LC3, average deceleration <= 22 g (India local sled margin); (R7) under LC4, AIP + bolt region max von Mises <= 184 MPa. Primary verification: explicit dynamic FEA for LC1, LC2, LC3 with crushable-foam material card (LS-DYNA MAT_083 / *MAT_FU_CHANG_FOAM or ABAQUS CRUSHABLE_FOAM); linear static for LC4. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Output one STEP AP242 assembly (foam core + skin + AIP) plus a report listing IA total mass, peak and average deceleration per case, crush stroke per case, absorbed energy per case, and AIP bolt-region von Mises under LC4.
short_prompt:
Design a foam-filled single-piece bolt-on Impact Attenuator for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 car per SUPRA 2026 v1.1: 300 kg at 7.0 m/s into the AIP, average <= 20 g, peak <= 40 g over any 3 ms window, energy absorbed >= 7350 J. India sled rig uses a 1018 steel surface at 0.12 friction (pre-measured), 28 C ambient.

Envelope: 200 x 100 x 200 mm IMPAXX 700 EPS core (29 kg/m^3, plateau 0.31 MPa to 70% strain, ultimate 70%), 1.2 mm 6061-T6 skin, bonded to AIP with a 15 mm ring of 3M DP420; AIP 1.5 mm 6061-T6 200 x 350 mm, bolted with 4 x M8 on a 180 x 150 mm pattern.

Factor regime: explicit-dynamics pass/fail on crush; local AIP + bolt yield FoS 1.5 for the static bolt-pull case; crush-stroke <= 170 mm (30 mm margin).

Load cases.
- Regulatory frontal: 300 kg at 7.0 m/s vs rigid barrier.
- Oblique 10 deg: 300 kg at 7.0 m/s.
- India sled-test correlation: 300 kg at 6.5 m/s against a 0.12 friction sled surface (local margin avg <= 22 g).
- Bolt pull: 4 kN steady tension per AIP bolt.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Foam crush at 7 m/s is explicit dynamic.

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 foam IA
prompt.brief: IMPAXX-700 foam IA meeting SUPRA 2026 v1.1 IA rule (200x100x200 mm, 20 g avg / 40 g peak, 7350 J) with India sled-test correlation at 0.12 friction.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: India 28 C ambient is within foam spec window.
